preamble: The voiced alveolar flap, often an allophone of /r/, can undergo vocalization to a close-mid front unrounded vowel in certain phonetic environments, particularly in coda positions. This transformation involves the consonant losing its consonantal features and assimilating to a vowel, specifically a front unrounded vowel.
phoneticEffects: Vocalization, Consonant-to-Vowel Shift, Rhotic Vocalization, Fronting

examples.note: Vocalization of coda /r/ (which can be realized as a flap [ɾ]) to [e] in the word for 'gate'.

sources: Noelliste, E. (2019). Bavarian German r-Flapping: Evidence for a dialect-specific sonority hierarchy. Glossa: a journal of general linguistics, 4(1): 79. https://doi.org/10.5334/gjgl.789
